watchlist.name: Lumentum Holdings
watchlist.thesis: AI era 'light supplier' — makes EML laser chips (50-60% global share), 1.6T optical modules, and OCS optical circuit switches. Demand exceeds supply by 25-30%. Management guides $2B/quarter ($8B annualized) revenue at 40%+ operating margin. $1500 = $8B × 40% × 83% ÷ 74M shares × 42 P/E = $1,508.
watchlist.kill_condition: Gross margin stuck below 40% for 2 quarters OR management says 'supply normalizing' on EML OR AI capex cut by 2+ hyperscalers

target.notes: P/E 42x from NVIDIA/Arista peer range for high-growth AI infra. Revenue $8B (mgmt target), Op margin 40% (mgmt mid-term), Tax 17% (actual FY2026), Shares 74M (fully diluted incl. convertibles + NVIDIA preferred).

bull.trigger: OCS exceeds $10B/yr, gross margin breaks 50%, revenue $9-10B, margin 42-45%

base.trigger: Management guidance met: $8B revenue, 40% op margin, 42x P/E

bear.trigger: AI capex slows, revenue $6-6.5B, P/E compresses to 30-35x

criteria.label: Gross margin trending toward 48%+
criteria.detail: Currently 42.5%, management target 50.5%. Each 1 ppt increase lifts fair value. Key threshold: break 48% by FY2027 (June 2027). If stuck at 40%, $1500 thesis severely weakened.
criteria.eval_hint: Check latest quarterly gross margin from earnings. Met if ≥46% or trending up ≥2 ppt QoQ toward 48%.

criteria.label: OCS revenue reaches 15-20% of total
criteria.detail: $4B+ order book converting. When OCS breaks 15-20% of quarterly revenue, market re-rates Lumentum from 'components company' to 'systems company' — completely different valuation level.
criteria.eval_hint: Check OCS revenue as % of total quarterly revenue from earnings breakdown. Met if OCS ≥15% of total.

criteria.label: QoQ revenue growth sustains 15%+
criteria.detail: Q1→Q2 was 24.7%, Q2→Q3 guided ~21%. Sustaining ≥15% QoQ gets to $2B/quarter target ahead of schedule. Any sequential decline (even 1-2%) is a major red flag.
criteria.eval_hint: Calculate QoQ revenue growth from last 2 quarterly revenue figures. Met if ≥15%.

criteria.label: EML supply gap maintained, pricing power intact
criteria.detail: 25-30% demand exceeds supply. On earnings calls listen for: (1) supply gap size and (2) ASP/pricing mentions. WARNING: if management says 'supply returning to balance', pricing power is weakening.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for management commentary on EML demand/supply dynamics in earnings calls or news. Met if demand still exceeds supply by 15%+.

criteria.label: Fully diluted shares stay below 80M
criteria.detail: $3.18B convertible bonds + NVIDIA preferred stock could convert. If shares exceed 80M, EPS gets diluted and $1500 becomes significantly harder. Watch convertible triggers closely.
criteria.eval_hint: Check diluted share count from latest 10-Q. Met if fully diluted shares ≤80M.

criteria.label: 2nd and 3rd customers exceed 10% revenue
criteria.detail: Historically dependent on one customer (Google). Disclosure of 2nd and 3rd 10%+ revenue customers = lower concentration risk = higher justified P/E.
criteria.eval_hint: Check 10-K/10-Q customer concentration disclosures. Met if ≥2 customers above 10% revenue.

criteria.label: CPO order execution on track or early
criteria.detail: Multi-hundred-million CPO orders planned for H1 2027 delivery. Early delivery or additional CPO orders = leading indicator that CPO business will explode ahead of analyst models.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for CPO delivery timeline updates or new CPO order announcements in earnings/news. Met if deliveries on or ahead of schedule.

criteria.label: Opex growth < 50% of revenue growth for 3 quarters
criteria.detail: Q2 operating margin expanded 1700 bps YoY — this operating leverage separates '$1000 stock' from '$1500 stock'. If opex growth stays below half of revenue growth rate, profitability explosion incoming.
criteria.eval_hint: Compare opex growth rate vs revenue growth rate over last 3 quarters. Met if opex growth < 50% of revenue growth consistently.

watchlist.name: Palantir Technologies
watchlist.thesis: AIP platform creating a new category of AI-driven enterprise software. Government contracts provide stable base, US commercial growth at 137% YoY is explosive. FY2026 guided $7.2B revenue at 57% adj operating margin. If AIP adoption follows CRM-like S-curve, $500+ is achievable.
watchlist.kill_condition: US commercial revenue growth drops below 50% YoY for 2 quarters OR adjusted operating margin falls below 35%

target.notes: P/E 65x justified by 61% revenue growth guidance, 57% adj operating margin, AIP-driven category creation. Revenue $12B (FY2028E at 40% CAGR from $7.2B), Op margin 40% GAAP, Tax 21%, Shares 2600M.

bull.trigger: AIP adoption exceeds expectations, US commercial 150%+ growth, operating margin 45%+, revenue $15B+

base.trigger: Revenue $12B by FY2028, 40% GAAP operating margin, P/E 65x sustained

bear.trigger: AIP adoption slows, growth decelerates to 30%, P/E compresses to 40x

criteria.label: US commercial revenue growth sustains 80%+ YoY
criteria.detail: Q4 2025 was 137% YoY. This is the single most important metric — proves AIP is not just hype. Must sustain ≥80% for the next 4 quarters. Deceleration to 50% seriously wounds the thesis.
criteria.eval_hint: Check quarterly US commercial revenue YoY growth from earnings. Met if ≥80% YoY.

criteria.label: Adjusted operating margin stays above 55%
criteria.detail: Q4 2025 adj operating margin was 57%. This validates Palantir's software-level economics. Sustained 55%+ proves AIP scales without proportional cost increases.
criteria.eval_hint: Check adjusted operating margin from quarterly earnings. Met if ≥55%.

criteria.label: AIP customer count growing 50%+ YoY
criteria.detail: AIP bootcamp → production conversion rate and net-new AIP customers are the leading indicators. More customers = more durable growth, less concentration risk.
criteria.eval_hint: Check total customer count or AIP-specific customer count growth from earnings. Met if 50%+ YoY growth.

criteria.label: FY2026 revenue guidance maintained or raised above $7.2B
criteria.detail: Initial FY2026 guidance was $7.18-7.20B (61% YoY). Any guidance raise signals management confidence. A cut would be devastating to the multiple.
criteria.eval_hint: Check latest FY2026 revenue guidance from earnings. Met if guidance ≥$7.2B and not lowered.

criteria.label: Government revenue growth stays positive (15%+ YoY)
criteria.detail: Government is the stable base. If DOGE-related budget cuts hit Palantir's government contracts, it removes the floor under the thesis. Needs to stay growing, even if slowly.
criteria.eval_hint: Check government segment revenue YoY growth from earnings. Met if ≥15% YoY.

criteria.label: Adjusted FCF exceeds $4B annualized
criteria.detail: FY2026 guidance: adj FCF $3.9-4.1B. Exceeding $4B proves the business is a genuine cash machine, not just an accounting story. FCF/share is the ultimate validation.
criteria.eval_hint: Check trailing 4Q adjusted free cash flow from earnings or 10-K. Met if ≥$4B annualized.

criteria.label: International revenue re-accelerates to 25%+ YoY
criteria.detail: International has been the weak spot. If AIP drives international acceleration, it's a massive TAM expansion signal. Currently lagging US growth significantly.
criteria.eval_hint: Check international revenue YoY growth from earnings. Met if ≥25% YoY.

criteria.label: Stock-based comp stays below 15% of revenue
criteria.detail: SBC has been a persistent concern. If SBC stays below 15% of revenue, GAAP profitability improves and dilution concerns ease. Above 20% is a red flag.
criteria.eval_hint: Check SBC as % of revenue from quarterly financials. Met if SBC < 15% of revenue.

watchlist.name: Rocket Lab USA
watchlist.thesis: Only credible SpaceX competitor. Electron is the proven small-launch workhorse (50+ launches). Neutron medium-lift rocket targeting Q4 2026 first flight unlocks $10B+ addressable market. Space Systems (66% of revenue) diversifies with $1.85B backlog including $816M SDA contract.
watchlist.kill_condition: Neutron first flight delayed beyond mid-2027 OR catastrophic test failure OR backlog drops below $1B

target.notes: Pre-profit, so P/E is aspirational FY2028 target. Revenue $2.5B (backlog conversion + Neutron), Op margin 15% first-time profitability, Tax 21%, Shares 580M. High P/E justified by SpaceX-like scarcity premium.

bull.trigger: Neutron flies on time, wins 3+ commercial contracts, Space Systems backlog $3B+, revenue $3B+

base.trigger: Neutron flies Q4 2026, revenue $2.5B by FY2028, first GAAP profit

bear.trigger: Neutron delayed to 2028, continued losses, dilution, backlog stalls

criteria.label: Neutron first flight achieved by Q4 2026
criteria.detail: This is THE binary catalyst. Tank test failure pushed timeline to Q4 2026. Successful first flight unlocks medium-launch TAM and validates $200+ thesis. Delay to 2028 = bear case.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for Neutron test flight announcements, launch date updates, or management commentary on timeline. Met if first flight completed or imminent (within 30 days).

criteria.label: Backlog exceeds $2.5B
criteria.detail: Ended 2025 at $1.85B (+73% YoY). Backlog is the best forward indicator. $2.5B+ signals strong demand even before Neutron flies. 37% of backlog converts within 12 months.
criteria.eval_hint: Check total backlog figure from quarterly earnings. Met if backlog ≥$2.5B.

criteria.label: Revenue growth sustains 50%+ YoY
criteria.detail: Q1 2026 guided $185-200M (57% YoY growth). Sustaining 50%+ proves the business is scaling independently of Neutron. FY2025 was $602M (+38% YoY).
criteria.eval_hint: Check quarterly revenue YoY growth from earnings. Met if ≥50% YoY.

criteria.label: Electron launch cadence reaches 25+ per year
criteria.detail: 2025 had 21 launches. Increasing cadence proves operational maturity and drives launch services revenue. Target 25+ in 2026, building toward 30+ by 2027.
criteria.eval_hint: Count Electron launches YTD and annualize. Met if on pace for 25+ launches in current year.

criteria.label: Non-GAAP gross margin reaches 40%+
criteria.detail: Q1 2026 guided 39-41% non-GAAP. Breaking above 40% consistently shows improving unit economics even as SDA lower-margin programs scale up.
criteria.eval_hint: Check non-GAAP gross margin from quarterly earnings. Met if ≥40%.

criteria.label: Space Systems wins 2+ new prime contracts
criteria.detail: SDA Tranche III ($816M) was a breakthrough. Additional prime contracts beyond SDA prove Rocket Lab is a credible space systems company, not just a launch provider.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for new prime contract announcements from Rocket Lab in news/press releases. Met if 2+ new prime contracts since Jan 2026.

criteria.label: Neutron secures 3+ commercial launch contracts pre-flight
criteria.detail: Pre-flight commercial demand for Neutron validates market need. SpaceX backlog proves demand exists — Neutron just needs to credibly offer an alternative.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for Neutron-specific contract announcements. Met if 3+ commercial customers have signed for Neutron launches.

criteria.label: Cash runway exceeds 18 months without dilutive raise
criteria.detail: Rocket Lab burns cash while scaling. If they need another equity raise before Neutron revenue starts, dilution hits shareholders. Monitor cash + credit facility headroom.
criteria.eval_hint: Check cash position and quarterly burn rate from 10-Q. Met if cash / quarterly burn ≥ 6 quarters remaining.

watchlist.name: Archer Aviation
watchlist.thesis: First-mover in commercial eVTOL with 100% FAA Means of Compliance acceptance for Midnight aircraft. $6B indicative order backlog (1,200 aircraft), United Airlines $1.5B conditional purchase, Stellantis manufacturing partnership. $2B cash runway. First commercial flights targeted 2026.
watchlist.kill_condition: FAA Type Certificate delayed beyond Q2 2027 OR cash burn exceeds $800M/year without revenue ramp OR United Airlines cancels order

target.notes: Pre-revenue event-driven. FY2028E: 200 aircraft delivered at $3.5M each = $700M revenue, 10% operating margin (early production), Tax 0% (NOL usage), ~500M shares. P/E 50x for category-creation premium. Probability-weighted.

bull.trigger: FAA cert by mid-2026, Abu Dhabi + US routes live, 300+ aircraft orders converting, revenue $1B+ run rate

base.trigger: FAA cert Q4 2026, limited commercial ops begin, 100-200 aircraft in production pipeline

bear.trigger: FAA cert delayed to 2028, cash burn accelerates, major dilutive raise needed, order cancellations

criteria.label: FAA Type Certificate received for Midnight
criteria.detail: 100% Means of Compliance accepted. Type Inspection Authorization (TIA) expected 2026. The Type Certificate is the single gate to commercial operations. Everything else is noise until this happens.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for FAA Type Certificate or TIA announcement for Archer Midnight. Met if TC granted or TIA activities underway with clear timeline.

criteria.label: First revenue-generating commercial flight completed
criteria.detail: Abu Dhabi is the initial target market. First passenger-carrying revenue flight proves the product works commercially. Targeted for 2026.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for news about Archer's first commercial/revenue flight operations. Met if at least one revenue flight has occurred.

criteria.label: Cash runway exceeds 18 months at current burn
criteria.detail: Ended 2025 with $2B cash, but Q1 2026 EBITDA loss guided $160-180M. At $180M/quarter burn, runway is ~11 quarters. Must not need dilutive raise before revenue starts.
criteria.eval_hint: Calculate: cash / quarterly adjusted EBITDA loss from latest financials. Met if ≥6 quarters of runway remaining.

criteria.label: United Airlines converts conditional order to firm order
criteria.detail: The $1.5B conditional purchase agreement becomes a firm order once Midnight gets FAA certification. Conversion de-risks the revenue pipeline dramatically.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for United Airlines firm order conversion announcement. Met if conditional order converted to firm/binding agreement.

criteria.label: Stellantis manufacturing facility operational
criteria.detail: Stellantis partnership provides manufacturing scale. Facility must be producing aircraft at rate to meet order backlog. Without manufacturing scale, orders can't convert to revenue.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for Stellantis/Archer manufacturing facility updates, production rate announcements. Met if facility is producing aircraft.

criteria.label: 2+ additional city/region launch contracts signed
criteria.detail: Abu Dhabi is first. Additional cities (US cities, India, etc.) expand the addressable market and prove global demand. Each new city = incremental TAM.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for new city/region partnership or launch contract announcements. Met if 2+ new markets announced beyond Abu Dhabi.

criteria.label: 500+ flight test hours accumulated
criteria.detail: More flight hours = more data = faster certification path. Also builds confidence in reliability. Track cumulative flight hours from press releases.
criteria.eval_hint: Check cumulative flight test hours from Archer press releases or earnings. Met if ≥500 hours accumulated.

criteria.label: Maintains 6+ month lead over Joby in certification
criteria.detail: Joby Aviation is the primary competitor. If Archer maintains its certification lead, first-mover advantage compounds. If Joby catches up, the premium narrows.
criteria.eval_hint: Compare Archer vs Joby FAA certification milestones from news. Met if Archer has clear timeline lead in certification.

watchlist.name: Celsius Holdings
watchlist.thesis: Third-largest US energy drink brand with ~20% market share. $2.5B revenue (2025). Pepsi distribution partnership as energy category captain. International approaching $100M run rate with expansion into Australia, UK, France. Gross margin improving to low 50s%. Forward P/E ~38x with growth premium.
watchlist.kill_condition: US market share loss for 2 consecutive quarters OR gross margin drops below 45% OR Pepsi partnership restructured negatively

target.notes: Consumer staples growth company. Revenue $3.5B (FY2027E), Op margin 18% (mature brand + scale), Tax 23%, Shares 260M. P/E 38x for category disruptor with Pepsi distribution moat.

bull.trigger: International revenue $300M+, US share 25%+, gross margin 55%+, operating margin 22%+

base.trigger: Revenue $3.5B, operating margin 18%, sustained 20% market share, P/E 38x

bear.trigger: Market share erodes to 16%, international stalls, gross margin stuck at 48%, P/E compresses to 25x

criteria.label: US energy drink market share holds 20%+ (dollar share)
criteria.detail: Reached 20% in Q4 2025. Maintaining or growing share against Monster and Red Bull validates the brand. Two consecutive quarters of share loss triggers kill condition.
criteria.eval_hint: Check US energy drink dollar market share from Nielsen/IRI data referenced in earnings. Met if ≥20%.

criteria.label: Gross margin stabilizes at 50%+ (low 50s)
criteria.detail: Currently 50.4%, management targeting low 50s as Alani Nu integration completes. Sustained 50%+ validates pricing power and integration success.
criteria.eval_hint: Check gross margin from quarterly earnings. Met if ≥50% for 2 consecutive quarters.

criteria.label: Revenue growth sustains 20%+ YoY (organic)
criteria.detail: Organic growth (excluding Alani Nu acquisition) must stay 20%+ to justify growth premium P/E. If growth slows to 10-15%, P/E compresses to Monster-like 25-28x.
criteria.eval_hint: Check organic revenue growth (ex-acquisition) from earnings commentary. Met if ≥20% YoY organic.

criteria.label: International revenue exceeds $150M annualized
criteria.detail: International approaching $100M run rate. Breaking $150M proves the brand travels globally. Australia, UK, France leading. Each new geography = incremental TAM.
criteria.eval_hint: Check international segment revenue from quarterly earnings, annualize. Met if ≥$150M annualized.

criteria.label: Pepsi distribution metrics improving (ACV, distribution points)
criteria.detail: As Pepsi's energy category captain, Celsius should see expanding ACV (all-commodity volume) and new distribution points. This is the moat — Monster has Coke, Celsius has Pepsi.
criteria.eval_hint: Check ACV or distribution point metrics from earnings calls. Met if ACV or distribution growing QoQ.

criteria.label: Operating margin reaches 15%+
criteria.detail: Net profit margin was only 3% in 2025 (acquisition costs). As integration completes, operating margin should normalize to 15%+ by H2 2026. This is the profitability unlock.
criteria.eval_hint: Check GAAP operating margin from quarterly earnings. Met if ≥15%.

criteria.label: Alani Nu integration complete with margin accretion
criteria.detail: Alani Nu acquisition created short-term margin pressure. Successful integration means combined portfolio margin ≥ pre-acquisition levels. Two billion-dollar brands under one roof.
criteria.eval_hint: Look for management commentary on Alani Nu integration completion, margin impact. Met if integration complete and combined gross margin ≥50%.

watchlist.name: SanDisk Corporation
watchlist.thesis: NAND flash super-cycle beneficiary — spun off from Western Digital Feb 2025. AI-driven storage demand structural through 2028+. Q3 FY2026 guided $4.4-4.8B revenue at 65-67% gross margin. Forward P/E only 9.76×. $1500 = $25B revenue × 45% op margin × 88% after-tax ÷ 157M shares × 24 P/E = $1,513.
watchlist.kill_condition: NAND ASP declines for 2 consecutive quarters OR gross margin drops below 45% OR AI storage capex cuts by 2+ hyperscalers

target.notes: P/E 24x — premium to current 9.76× forward PE, justified by structural AI demand + supply discipline + margin expansion. Revenue $25B (FY2027E from Q3 run-rate growth), Op margin 45% (gross 65% - 20% opex), Tax 12% (semi tax credits), Shares 157M diluted.

bull.trigger: NAND shortage extends through 2028, revenue $30B+, gross margin 70%+, op margin 50%+, P/E re-rates to 30× as market recognizes structural moat

base.trigger: Revenue $25B (FY2027), op margin 45%, 24× P/E, sustained AI storage demand

bear.trigger: NAND cycle peaks mid-2027, ASP declines, revenue $18B, margin compresses to 30%, P/E 14×

criteria.label: Gross margin sustains 65%+ (structural, not cyclical)
criteria.detail: Q3 FY2026 guided 65-67% gross margin, up from 51.1% in Q2. If 65%+ holds for 2+ quarters, validates structural NAND pricing power vs cyclical spike. Key for $1500 thesis.
criteria.eval_hint: Check non-GAAP gross margin from quarterly earnings. Met if ≥65% for 2 consecutive quarters.

criteria.label: Sequential revenue growth sustains 20%+ QoQ
criteria.detail: Q1→Q2 was 31% sequential growth. Q3 guided ~50% sequential growth ($4.6B vs $3.03B). Sustaining 20%+ QoQ means $25B annualized run rate achievable by mid-FY2027.
criteria.eval_hint: Calculate QoQ revenue growth from last 2 quarters. Met if ≥20% sequential.

criteria.label: Data center revenue exceeds 25% of total
criteria.detail: Data center segment was $440M in Q2 (up 64% QoQ). AI/data center becoming dominant growth driver. Higher data center mix = higher margins + more durable demand.
criteria.eval_hint: Check data center segment revenue as % of total from earnings breakdown. Met if ≥25%.

criteria.label: Industry NAND supply remains constrained (no major capacity additions)
criteria.detail: Current NAND shortage is structural — supply discipline across Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron, SanDisk. Any major capacity expansion announcement would break the thesis.
criteria.eval_hint: Check for NAND capacity expansion announcements from Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron. Failed if any major new fab announced.

criteria.label: Free cash flow margin sustains 25%+ (capital discipline)
criteria.detail: Q2 adjusted FCF margin was 27.9%. Sustained 25%+ FCF proves the company can generate cash without massive capex reinvestment, unlike historical NAND cycles.
criteria.eval_hint: Check adjusted free cash flow / revenue from quarterly earnings. Met if ≥25%.

criteria.label: Forward P/E expands from 9.76× toward 15-20×
criteria.detail: Currently trading at 9.76× forward PE — well below semiconductor median. As market recognizes structural (not cyclical) nature of NAND demand, P/E should re-rate toward 15-20×. $1500 needs 24×.
criteria.eval_hint: Check forward PE from financial data. Met if forward PE ≥15×.
